core_mechanism: A stochastic contact/branching spreading process on a network of interacting agents, controlled by a tunable interaction-suppression parameter that crosses a critical threshold separating a supercritical (self-sustaining, growing) regime from a subcritical (dying-out, contained) regime
brief_reason: The abstract reports an 'important transition' in the interaction-suppression (social-distancing compliance) parameter between the 70-80% range, below which the spread persists and above which (90%) it is driven to extinction within a finite time, which is a critical-threshold branching/contact process on the agent network.
